summary: Stellar Scarlet>Fire and strong Desolation Row with solid jams
blurb: The show shines on a powerful Desolation Row and a seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition, while Women Are Smarter delivers an iconic Weir “F” bomb. Jerry’s guitar tone remains solid despite a minor lyric slip, and Phil’s bass glows on Box of Rain. The encore’s Not Fade Away ignites crowd chanting.
review:
May 10, 1987 at Laguna Seca captured the Dead in fine form. The first set builds momentum with a heartfelt Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo that leads into an inspired Box of Rain and a soaring Desolation Row – Bob’s vocal phrasing on the latter has been called “one of the greatest” by listeners. The second set is where the night truly clicks: Scarlet Begonias flows effortlessly into Fire on the Mountain, creating a high‑energy Scarlet > Fire sequence that many reviewers cite as a highlight. Women Are Smarter (Man Smart, Woman Smarter) erupts with Bob’s famous “F” bomb and a punchy drum/Space segment, while the unexpected Playin’ in the Band jam adds an extra layer of improvisational fire. The encore caps it off with a raucous Not Fade Away that sent the crowd chanting into the night. Across the board the soundboard recordings are clear, though a tinny edge and occasional channel blow‑outs during Space are noted; nevertheless the performance itself remains consistently engaging.

Jerry’s guitar work is solid throughout, marred only by a lyric flub on Fire, while Phil provides a sweet, fragile bass line that shines especially on Box of Rain. Bob’s rhythm cuts through with a chime‑like quality and his vocal delivery on Desolation Row stands out as a show‑stopper. Brent’s keyboards are in the mix but receive no specific accolades in the reviews, and both Mickey and Bill keep the rhythmic foundation tight – Mickey’s drum channel does blow out during Space, yet the post‑drums segment is praised for its strength. Overall this night offers a well‑rounded set of classics and improvisations that make it worth revisiting.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive (Desolation Row, Scarlet>Fire, Women Are Smarter praised) while the other three are mixed but contain no consistent negative criticism of the performance itself. Positive language such as "powerful", "iconic", and "solid" appears across multiple sources, justifying a moderate +1.0 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard source with good overall clarity, captures the standout songs (Women Are Smarter, Box of Rain, Desolation Row, Scarlet>Fire) and has the most complete, listenable tape.

key_highlights: Desolation Row delivers a powerful vocal and instrumental performance.
Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segues seamlessly with high energy.
Women Are Smarter features an iconic Weir “F” bomb and strong drum/Space segment.
Not Fade Away encore sparks crowd chanting and caps the night.

band_performance.Jerry: Solid guitar tone throughout with a minor lyric slip on Fire; overall his playing stays tight and expressive.
band_performance.Phil: Sweet, fragile bass shines especially on Box of Rain; clear and loud presence in the mix.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar cuts through with chime‑like quality; vocal phrasing on Desolation Row praised as a highlight, plus an iconic “F” bomb in Women Are Smarter.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums channel blows out during Space but the post‑drums segment is strong and well received.
